title: About Me
text:
🏠 Dashboard
  🎧 Mood of the Week
  ℹ️ About


  ℹ️ About Me
  📊 Hot 100 Chart
  🎧 Mood Snapshot













Hi, I’m Mario

Welcome to my project!
This dashboard is part of a personal learning end to end analytics project. Interacting with multiple apis, ingesting, cleaning, analyzing real-time data. 
The first page of this dashboard was inspired by Melissa Van Bussel’s 2024 Posit Table Contest submission, which served as a template and educational reference.
The current version includes a lightweight data pipeline that refreshes Spotify Hot 100 track data, manages cache files for weekly updates, and enriches chart tracks with audio features for analysis-ready outputs. Second page will be added soon, and will include a more in depth analysis of the audio features of the tracks, as well as some visualizations. Taking a look at how music has evolved over time, and how the audio features of the tracks have changed over time.
If you’d like to connect, you can find me here:


GitHub: github.com/MariojReyes

LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/marioreyesiii

title: Mood of the Week — Billboard Hot 100
text:
ℹ️ About Me 📊 Hot 100 Chart 🎧 Mood Snapshot


Mood Index is a composite score from valence, energy, danceability, and tempo (scaled 0–100), shown as both track-average and popularity-weighted exposure.


KPIs


       Mood Index
       56.1
       55.9 weighted
     
       Avg Valence
       0.471
       0.469 weighted
     
       Avg Energy
       0.628
       0.624 weighted
     
       Median Tempo
       125 BPM

Song Preview


hot100 = await FileAttachment("data/hot100_ojs.json").json()

viewof songDropdown = Inputs.select(
  hot100,
  {
    label: "Choose a song",
    format: d => `${d.song_name} — ${d.artist_name}`,
    unique: true
  }
)
















html`<iframe
  style="border-radius:12px;margin-top:10px;display:block;"
  src=${`https://open.spotify.com/embed/track/${songDropdown.song_id}?utm_source=generator&theme=0`}
  width="100%"
  height="180"
  frameBorder="0"
  allowfullscreen=""
  allow="autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; fullscreen; picture-in-picture"
  loading="lazy">
</iframe>`







Mood is inferred from Spotify audio features — an aggregate signal of track acoustics, not direct listener emotion. Popularity weighting proxies exposure. Tracking started March 15, 2026.
